Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple [An expert-level threat actor group C0018 targets a healthcare organization using a simple yet effective approach. They exploit a public-facing application to gain initial access, execute malicious commands, and ultimately cause impact through data encryption.]
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Internal Network, File System
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Command Line Interface

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit an unpatched vulnerability in a public-facing healthcare application server, allowing them unauthorized access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers utilize legitimate system tools or scripts to execute commands, further compromising the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers deploy AvosLocker ransomware to encrypt critical data across the file system, rendering it inaccessible until a ransom is paid.

Scenario.summary: [In a targeted attack against a healthcare provider, the sophisticated group C0018 exploited a vulnerable public-facing application to gain entry. Leveraging system scripting interpreters, they executed commands to prepare for a devastating impact: encrypting sensitive files with ransomware. This operation exemplifies a straightforward yet potent strategy aimed at financial extortion, exploiting the sector's reliance on data integrity.]
